BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after the landlord mentioned he might sell the building and raise the lease at renewal. Before year-end review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Revisit after year-end once 2025 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~15-16 class seats per month at the current cost structure. With classes, wholesale, commissions and adjunct teaching there is comfortable headroom. Main risk is a rent jump if the landlord sells the building (lease runs through March 2027).
ActionItems: Ask the landlord to put any renewal terms in writing before the March 2027 lease end
Revisit after year-end with updated 2025 actuals
